meta.title: Mount Kamifuse Ski Area
meta.description: Honshu's northernmost ski hill with Mutsu Bay panoramas, cat pudding cafe, and night skiing.

hero.title:
Mount
Kamifuse
hero.tagline: Ski toward the sea at Honshu's northern edge—a rare ocean-view downhill.

transit.title:
Where the road
meets the bay.
transit.lead: About 15 minutes from Ominato IC. Mutsu Bay and the naval port unfold below—a downhill run you won't find elsewhere. A natural base for Shimokita drives.
transit.imgAlt: Snowy coastal road overlooking Mutsu Bay—approach to Kamifuse

primary.title: Downhill toward the ocean
primary.lead: On clear days, panoramas stretch from the Hakkoda range toward Hokkaido. Mutsu Bay and Ominato port lie right below—a rare seaside downhill in Japan.
primary.imgAlt: Groomed slope overlooking Mutsu Bay at Kamifuse

secondary.title: Famous cat pudding cafe
secondary.lead: Re:lax+kitchen's cat-shaped pudding draws visitors who come just for the view and sweets. Weekday-only slots are common—check before you go.

recovery.title:
After skiing,
Ominato eats.
recovery.lead: Morning miso ramen at Arasoba (opens 7:00), butter sandwiches at Yoshida Bakery, navy croquettes at local izakaya—classic après routes.
recovery.whisper: Day-trip soaks at Mutsu Yatate Onsen or Tonan Onsen pair well with a ski day.
recovery.imgAlt: Steaming miso ramen in Ominato after skiing

journey.title:
A full day from
the port town
journey.lead: Combine ocean-view skiing, harbor food, maritime history, and night lights in one Shimokita loop.

01.title: Morning ramen
01.body: Miso ramen at Arasoba—a local early-bird staple

02.title: Bay-view runs
02.body: Downhill with Mutsu Bay below, or cat pudding at the center house

03.title: Harbor lunch
03.body: Yoshida Bakery butter sandwiches and seafood

04.title: Hoppokan museum
04.body: Maritime Self-Defense Force history—free entry (check winter hours)

05.title: Night lights & onsen
05.body: Mutsu's Agehacho city lights and Yatate Onsen to finish

wellness.title: Cat pudding & view cafe
wellness.body: Re:lax+kitchen cat pudding and blue cream soda—often weekday-only and sell out early. Lift tickets reach the view deck without gear.
wellness.thumbAlt: Cat pudding with snowy slope view through the lodge window

walk.title: 15 minutes from Ominato
walk.body: Gateway for Shimokita drives via Ominato IC. Studless tires required in winter—watch coastal road conditions.

responsible.title: Seaside slope etiquette
responsible.body: Walk between Lift 2 and Lift 1 (~50 m). Heavy wet snow can be tiring. Confirm lift and snow reports before you travel.

bluePond.title: Agehacho — Mutsu night lights
bluePond.body: City lights spread like butterfly wings over the bay—visible from night skiing on the hill.
bluePond.thumbAlt: Mutsu city lights at night—the Agehacho view

food.title: Ominato dining 10 — before & after
food.body: Miso ramen, navy croquettes, local seafood—harbor-town picks for your ski day.
food.thumbAlt: Ominato miso ramen—from morning to izakaya night

onsen.title: Nearby onsen 10 — snow-view soaks
onsen.body: Yatate, Okuyakken, and Tonan onsen—day-trip baths after skiing.

featuredSpot.title: Agehacho lights
featuredSpot.lead: Mutsu's butterfly-shaped city glow pairs with Kamifuse night skiing.
featuredSpot.imgAlt: City lights viewed from the snowy slope at night

meta.title: Shirogane Agehacho night view — Mount Kamifuse Ski Area
meta.description: Winter light-up, access, and pairing with snow play—guide for international visitors

hero.title:
Japan's Agehacho night view—
beyond the photo
hero.tagline: The cobalt blue the world knows—after snow wellness, into the winter night

bluePond.notice: Light-up dates, parking, and bus service change by season. Confirm on Kamifuse Town or tourism association official sites before you go.
bluePond.introLead: Shirogane Agehacho night view (Aoiike) was created as a by-product of erosion-control work linked to Mt. Tokachi volcanic activity.
bluePond.introBody: Minerals from submerged birch and fallen leaves scatter light into cobalt blue—a Kamifuse view where nature and human intervention overlap.
bluePond.whenLead: Typically from around November through April, lights come on after sunset. In crisp midwinter air, the blue sinks deep into still water—the hour overseas visitors seek most.
bluePond.whenNote: Viewing decks or parking may be restricted due to weather, snow, or crowds.
bluePond.accessTitle: From Kamifuse Station & the ski area
bluePond.accessLead: About 25 minutes by car from Kamifuse Station (Shirogane Onsen area). After the ski hill, most visitors go by rental car or winter bus/tour.

drive.label: Car
drive.body: Kamifuse Station → Shirogane Onsen area, approx. 25 min. Winter tires required.

bus.label: Bus & tours
bus.body: Seasonal buses and day tours from Kamifuse/Furano (schedules vary by year).

pair.label: Itinerary
pair.body: Ski area → warming lunch → Shirogane Onsen → Agehacho night view light-up in one day.

winter.label: Winter
winter.body: Watch for ice and blizzards. Tripods only in designated areas.

bluePond.responsibleLead: No trespassing on farmland—the same stance as our "landscape is livelihood" message. Shoot from the viewing deck, yield in crowds, and respect tripod etiquette.
bluePond.mapsBody: Shirogane, Kamifuse-cho, Kamikawa-gun, Hokkaido. Check your route on Google Maps.
bluePond.heroImageAlt: Shirogane Agehacho night view at night under light-up—birch silhouettes and cobalt water
bluePond.galleryAlt: Winter at Shirogane Agehacho night view—cobalt surface framed by snow

meta.title: Snow Play Experiences — Mount Kamifuse Ski Area
meta.description: Furari Winter Walk snowshoe tour, Tokotoko Adventure booking, and free snow play—official links included

hero.title:
Snow play
without skiing
hero.tagline: From a free snow plaza to guided hill snowshoes—touch Kamifuse winter

snowPlay.notice: Seasons, prices, and capacity change—each operator's official site is authoritative. Hill access may require a licensed guide (no trespass on farmland or private land).
snowPlay.introLead: Mount Kamifuse Ski Area offers free snow play. Go further with the tourism association's hill tour or local operator bookings.
snowPlay.introBody: Enjoy only in welcoming spaces—no entering patchwork farmland. Choose designated routes and guided experiences.
snowPlay.hubTitle: Town ski area snow play plaza
snowPlay.hubLead: About a 15-minute walk from JR Kamifuse Station. Sledding, snowmen, deep-snow play—a wellness-oriented free zone (lift free).
snowPlay.experiencesLead: For hill views and adventures, check the latest info and book on the official pages below.

furari.title: "Furari Winter Walk"
furari.subtitle: Scenic hill snowshoe in Kamifuse
furari.lead: Crisp air and marshmallow hills—a picture-book snowscape on beginner-friendly snowshoes. Meet at Shiki-no-Joho-kan next to JR Kamifuse Station.
furari.season: Typically late December–early March (varies with snow)
furari.meet: Shiki-no-Joho-kan (Kamifuse Tourism Association) · 1 min from JR Kamifuse Station
furari.note: Hill snowshoeing runs only on permitted guided tours. You cannot walk the hills without an authorized guide.

tokotoko.title: Tokotoko Adventure
tokotoko.subtitle: Book Kamifuse winter activities
tokotoko.lead: Snowshoe and snow experiences bookable online. Check availability and courses on the reservation site.

snowPlay.pairingLead: AM: Furari Winter Walk → Lunch near the station → PM: free snow play at the town ski area → Night: Shirogane Agehacho night view light-up.
snowPlay.heroImageAlt: Snowshoeing Kamifuse snowfields—a snow wellness experience
snowPlay.galleryAlt: White hills and clear winter sky—Kamifuse snow wellness scenery

meta.title: 10 Nearby Eats — Mount Kamifuse Ski Area
meta.description: Curated food picks and area map—pair with snow play and Agehacho night view in one day

hero.title:
After the snow,
into Hokkaido food
hero.tagline: Dairy, wagyu, wheat, ramen—pair iconic flavors with photo-ready locations for a seamless day

nearbyFood.notice: Hours, closures, and winter schedules change—each venue's official site is authoritative. No trespass on farmland; enjoy only in welcoming spaces.
nearbyFood.introLead: Visitors to Kamifuse—especially independent travel travelers—are highly sensitive to "only-in-Hokkaido" food and Instagram-ready settings.
nearbyFood.introBody: We picked ten strong food stops to hook travelers before and after snow scenes. Combined with the town ski area and Agehacho night view, they form a friction-free day-trip package.
nearbyFood.mapLead: Toggle food, onsen, and hub layers—show multiple at once.

mapLayers.hint: Food and onsen can be shown together
mapLayers.mobileHint: If pins overlap, use View on map in the list below or Spot list on the map

nearbyFood.spotsTitle: 10 nearby eats that resonate with Kamifuse visitors
nearbyFood.spotsLead: Grouped by category—easy to slot before or after snow play.

junpei.title: Yoshoku & Cafe Junpei
junpei.lead: A legendary western-style restaurant in Kamifuse. The signature shrimp fry bowl draws long queues from Asia-focused travelers.

biei-farm.title: Kamifuse Farm (grazing dairy)
biei-farm.lead: Vast pasture views plus raclette toast on Kamifuse wheat bread and fresh soft-serve—dairy and scenery in one stop.

aruno.title: Aruu no Pain
aruno.lead: Log-house bakery café. Hollowed house-made bread filled with cheese—the fondue set is a visual hit for photo hunters.

chiyoda.title: Farm Restaurant Chiyoda
chiyoda.lead: Farm-direct restaurant for wagyu fans—Kamifuse beef steak and hearty stews for meat-focused travelers.

ferme.title: Ferme La Terre Kamifuse
ferme.lead: "Live with nature" complex with beautiful architecture, refined souvenirs, and café-hopping appeal.

between.title: BETWEEN THE BREAD
between.lead: A burger shop alone in the forest. Takeout burgers with local ingredients—handy between drives and scenic stops.

gosh.title: Gosh Coffee Roastery
gosh.lead: Highly rated in Bibaushi—house-roasted coffee, bread, and antique interiors to warm up after hills and views.

asperge.title: Restaurant Asperges
asperge.lead: Inside JA Kamifuse’s Kamifuse Senka—French that maximizes fresh local produce for higher-budget food travelers.

sabo.title: Kamifuse Sabo
sabo.lead: Steps from Kamifuse Station. Kinako syrup soft-serve and drinks—Japanese dessert cues that hook overseas visitors.

santouka.title: Ramen Santouka Kamifuse
santouka.lead: Ramen is killer content . The famous Asahikawa-born chain on the Kamifuse route—essential after snow play.

nearbyFood.pairingLead: Kamifuse Station → snow play (town ski area) → lunch: Junpei or Kamifuse Sabo → afternoon: Kamifuse Farm or Aruu no Pain → night: Agehacho night view light-up. Sections work even without a rental car (walk + bus/taxi).

meta.title: 10 Nearby Onsen — Mount Kamifuse Ski Area
meta.description: Snow-view open-air baths and day visits—onsen map paired with snow play

hero.title:
Warm the body
in hot springs
hero.tagline: After snow play and Agehacho night view—Japanese onsen culture and snow-view roten

nearbyOnsen.notice: Day-bath availability, hours, winter closures, and mixed-bath rules change—check each facility. Use extra care at wild roten baths.
nearbyOnsen.introLead: visitors around Kamifuse strongly want Japanese onsen culture and open-air baths in snowy scenery.
nearbyOnsen.introBody: Ten baths that warm chilled bodies after snow play and scenic touring—designed as a digital bundle with our food map, like Japowserch set proposals.
nearbyOnsen.mapLead: Toggle food, onsen, and hub layers—show multiple at once.

mapLayers.hint: Food and onsen can be shown together
mapLayers.mobileHint: If pins overlap, use View on map in the list below or Spot list on the map

nearbyOnsen.spotsTitle: 10 nearby onsen that resonate with Kamifuse visitors
nearbyOnsen.spotsLead: Centered on Shirogane Onsen—easy to pair before/after snow play and Agehacho night view.

ao-no-bi-yuyu.title: Ao no Bi Yuyu (former Shirogane Hotel)
ao-no-bi-yuyu.lead: 100% free-flowing beautifying water with a stream-view open bath—an easy stop right after Agehacho night view.

park-hills.title: Hotel Park Hills Shirogane
park-hills.lead: The area's largest resort hotel near Shirahige Falls—alternating Japanese/Western baths and sauna for families and groups.

tsuewasure.title: Tsuewasure no Yu
tsuewasure.lead: No dilution, reheating, circulation, or chlorine—100% free-flowing. Cloudy iron-rich water for travelers seeking real local onsen.

mori-no-ryotei.title: Mori no Ryotei Kamifuse
mori-no-ryotei.lead: A 17-room sukiya-style luxury inn with day baths—Japanese aesthetics and hospitality for high-end guests.

mori-no-shizuku.title: Mori no Shizuku RIN
mori-no-shizuku.lead: 100% free-flowing roten, indoor baths, and sauna—clean, reliable comfort for international visitors.

kokumin.title: Kamifuse Kokumin Hoyojo Center
kokumin.lead: Public bath from around ¥300—unadorned local sento culture for travelers who want a deep, shareable experience.

ryounkaku.title: Tokachidake Onsen Ryounkaku (Kamifurano)
ryounkaku.lead: One of Hokkaido's highest onsen inns—iron-brown water and deep snow for ultimate photo contrast; hugely popular.

hakuginso.title: Fukiage Onsen Hakuginso (Kamifurano)
hakuginso.lead: Hiba tubs and open baths on Mt. Tokachi's mid-slope—sauna with aufguss, a pilgrimage site for sauna lovers.

fukiage-roten.title: Fukiage Roten no Yu (Kamifurano)
fukiage-roten.lead: A free 24-hour wild mixed bath in the forest—the ultimate adventure soak in snowy nature.

yukoma.title: Asahidake Onsen Yukomanso (Higashikawa)
yukoma.lead: A famed bath at the foot of Mt. Asahidake—wooden rustic tubs and a hub for powder skiers and mountain visitors.

nearbyOnsen.pairingTitle: Snow play × onsen × Agehacho night view
nearbyOnsen.pairingLead: AM: town ski snow play → lunch: Junpei or Kamifuse Sabo → PM: Shirogane onsen → evening: Agehacho night view → night: Ryounkaku or Yukomanso (stay extension).

access.note: ~15 min by car from Ominato IC · Mutsu City operated
access.rentacarEyebrow: 陸奥湾絶景・ねこプリン
access.rentacarLink: 七戸十和田駅でレンタカー予約
access.rentacarNote: スカイチケット（外部サイト）
access.rentacarHint: 釜臥山スキー場周辺のドライブ向け
